name: Perforin surveillance loss accelerated senescence and aging
summary: Perforin-deficient mice support the core premise that immune cytotoxicity helps restrain senescent-cell accumulation with age.
statement: Prf1-deficient mice with impaired cytotoxic immune surveillance accumulated more senescent cells and chronic inflammation, developed multiple age-related phenotypes with lower survival, and showed additional senescent-cell burden and shortened lifespan when crossed with a progeroid LMNA model.
population_or_model: Prf1 knockout mice and LMNA+/G609G progeroid mice
time_horizon: Aging cohorts through young, adult, and 24-month old mice plus progeroid survival analyses
quantitative_note: At 24 months, Prf1-deficient mice had up to 43% SA-beta-gal-positive cells in examined tissues versus around 10% in wild-type controls, with 2- to 4-fold higher burden depending on tissue.
caveats: This is loss-of-function immune biology, not a therapeutic enhancement trial.
ABT-737 rescue in the source is small-molecule senolysis, not immune clearance.
Perforin deficiency also affects cancer and infection surveillance, so some aging phenotypes may be indirect.
